data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Google's infrastructure uses advanced load balancing algorithms to distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers to ensure high availability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Use of global load balancers, Anycast technology to ensure low-latency and high-volume traffic management.

data.title: Monolithic and Microservices Integration
data.description: Google employs a combination of monolithic architecture for legacy systems and microservices for scalable, new services.
data.technical_spec: Kubernetes for container orchestration, gRPC for inter-service communication.

data.title: Scalable Data Storage
data.description: Google's architecture is designed to handle massive amounts of data with high scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Use of Google File System (GFS) and Bigtable for distributed storage and NoSQL solutions.

data.title: Global Data Centers
data.description: Numerous strategically located data centers worldwide to ensure low latency and redundancy.
data.technical_spec: Data centers use custom-built servers, efficient cooling systems, and renewable energy sources.

data.title: Front-end Web Optimization
data.description: Optimized front-end performance to enhance user experience with fast loading speeds and responsiveness.
data.technical_spec: Implementation of AMP and advanced caching mechanisms, HTTP/3 protocol usage.
